title: Information Technology Specialist (Data Management)
description: This position is located in the Department of Health & Human Services (HHS), Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS), Center for Medicaid and CHIP Services (CMCS), Data and Systems Group, Division of Information Systems As a Information Technology Specialist (Data Management), GS-2210-14, you will serve as a technical authority and advisor for the development of analytics products using Medicaid and CHIP datasets to support internal and external data product users.

title: Security Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: The Office of Protective Services located at Ames Research Center (ARC) Moffett Field, California, is looking for a dynamic professional to join our team as an Information and Industrial Security Specialist. This role involves the protection of classified and sensitive information; identification, classification and declassification of information; development of and provision of security training and review of contractor security classification data.

title: Computer Scientist, AST - Computer Research and Development (Direct Hire)
description: As a Computer Scientist in the Visualization group of the NASA Advanced Supercomputing Division, you will be responsible for architecting and supporting innovative high-performance computing and visualization solutions in support of various NASA missions and research and analysis in the broader research community. You will serve as an expert in the development and application of advanced visualization and data analysis techniques.

title: Management Analyst
description: As the Assessments, Cost Estimating, and Schedules Office Manager provides independent cost and schedule assessments that directly inform the International Space Station (ISS) Program Manager's decisions on appropriations and Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution process. The position ensures the accuracy, credibility, and transparency of financial and programmatic reporting to Agency leadership and Congress.

title: Budget Analyst
description: This position is part of the Office of Cybersecurity, Energy Security and Emergency Response, Department of Energy. As a Budget Analyst, you will be you will be provideing expertise in any aspect of DOE's budget process and analyzes a variety of highly complex DOE budgetary operations and functions in the assigned organization or program areas.

title: Facilities Project Manager
description: This position is part of the Office of Management, Department of Energy. As a Facilities Project Manager, you will be interpreting DOE-wide policies and developing, formulating and implementing for Headquarters policies, standards and procedures relating to space management and interior design activities.

title: Chief Project Management and Systems Engineer Division
description: Incumbent serves as the Chief of the Project Management and Systems Engineering (PM & SE) Division and reports to the Director of Engineering at Johnson Space Center (JSC). Provides overall management and leadership for human rated spacecraft design and development, vehicle systems definition and integration, overall vehicle testing and verification, vehicle sustaining engineering, logistics and maintenance, and development, delivery, and sustained operation of flight hardware.

title: Financial Management Specialist
description: This position is located in the DEA, Los Angeles Field Division. This position(s) primary purpose is to support the Domestic Division Financial Procurement Unit (FPU) with processing various types of accounting transactions, analyzing financial information, and providing customer support within the assigned Division offices.

title: Mission & Operations Support Specialist
description: This position is located in the DEA, Los Angeles Field Division, in Camarillo, California and Reno, Nevada. This position(s) primary purpose is to support the Outlying Office (OO) related to Business Operations and administrative aspects of Law Enforcement Operations.

title: IT SPECIALIST (PLCYPLN)
organization: Commander, Naval Information Warfare Systems Command (NAVWARSYSCOM)
description: This is a public notice flyer to notify interested applicants of anticipated vacancies. Applications will not be accepted through this flyer. Interested applicants must follow the directions in the "How to Apply" section of this flyer to be considered. There may or may not be actual vacancies filled from this flyer. Notice of Result letters will not be sent to applicants who respond to this flyer.

title: General Engineer, AST, Facility Systems Safety
description: As an AST, Facility System Safety Engineer within the Office of the Chief Safety and Mission Assurance Officer Division, you will serve as the System Safety Subject Matter Expert, for the Safety and Mission Assurance Directorate. You will provide overarching system safety philosophy, plans, coordination, evaluation, and monitoring related to engine test campaigns, facilities, systems, equipment, and operations.

title: Aerospace Engineer, AST, Aerospace Flight Systems (Direct Hire)
description: You will serve as an Aerospace Engineer, AST, with the Mechanical Engineering Branch within the Engineering and Test Directorate by supporting the design of facility test support systems (cryogenic, fluid, structural, high pressure liquid/gas, etc.) required to perform propulsion test activities at Stennis Space Center (SSC).

title: Logistics Management Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: You will serve as a Logistics Management Specialist in the Engineering and Test Directorate supporting the fabrication, assembly, testing, maintenance, and repair of facility ground support systems (cryogenic, fluid, electrical, structural, etc.) required to perform propulsion test activities at Stennis Space Center (SSC). These ground support systems are essential to the Center's efforts to support the Agency's Artemis efforts.

title: Logistics Management Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: You will serve as a Logistics Management Specialist in the Engineering and Test Directorate supporting the fabrication, assembly, testing, maintenance, and repair of facility ground support systems (cryogenic, fluid, electrical, structural, etc.) required to perform propulsion test activities at Stennis Space Center (SSC). These ground support systems are essential to the Center's efforts to support the Agency's Artemis efforts.

title: Accountant
description: This position is located in the DEA, Los Angeles, CA Field Division Office. This position(s) primary purpose is to accurately report the financial position of Division business activities for various internal and external stakeholders - including taxpayers, auditors, legislators, DEA executives, DEA financial managers, and others who may have a vested interest in the agency's financial activity.

title: General Engineer, AST, Experimental Facility Development (Direct Hire)
description: NASA Johnson Space Center is seeking a highly skilled Low Voltage Electrical Engineer to support mission-critical facilities and utilities infrastructure. This position serves as a technical authority responsible for ensuring the reliability, safety, design, and compliance of electrical systems supporting a large campus environment, including Central Heating and Cooling Plants and critical mission support systems

title: Engineering Technician (Direct Hire)
description: As an Engineering technician in the Engineering and Test Directorate, you will be an integral member of the team responsible for performing propulsion test activities at Stennis Space Center. Your skills will be highly utilized to fabricate, install, activate and operate technical systems necessary for the successful achievement of the goals of the Center, NASA and our commercial partners.

title: Quality Assurance Specialist (Aerospace) (Direct Hire)
description: Serves as Quality Assurance (QA)/safety specialist for fabrication, assembly, and operation of propulsion test facility support systems. Develops and reviews QA/safety procedures; conducts inspections and audits for compliance; monitors materials, calibration, nonconformances, and trends affecting test readiness; identifies hazards and implements required controls, conducts root cause analysis, develops corrective actions, and provides safety guidance to the workforce.

title: Lead Quality Assurance Specialist (Aerospace) (Direct Hire)
description: Serves as Lead Quality Assurance (QA)/safety specialist for fabrication, assembly, and operation of propulsion test facility support systems. Provides guidance on QA/safety procedures; coordinates and conducts inspections and audits for compliance; monitors materials, calibration, nonconformances, and trends affecting test readiness; identifies hazards and implements required controls, conducts root cause analysis, develops corrective actions, and provides safety guidance to the workforce.

title: Supervisory Quality Assurance Specialist (Aerospace) (Direct Hire)
description: Serves as Supervisor for Quality Assurance (QA)/safety specialist for fabrication, assembly, and operation of propulsion test facility support systems. Supervises employees who provide guidance on QA/safety procedures; conducts inspections and audits for compliance; monitors materials, calibration, nonconformances, and trends affecting test readiness; identifies hazards and implements required controls, conducts root cause analysis, develops corrective actions, and provides safety guidance.

title: Mechanical Engineer, AST, Mechanical Experimental Equipment (Direct Hire)
description: You will serve as an AST Mechanical Engineer within the Engineering and Test Directorate, supporting the design, testing, and operation of technical systems including cryogenic, fluid, structural, and high-pressure liquid/gas systems required to conduct propulsion test activities at NASA's Stennis Space Center.

title: Mechanical Engineer, AST, Mechanical Experimental Equipment (Direct Hire)
description: You will serve as a Mechanical Engineer within the Operations Division of the Engineering and Test Directorate, supporting the development, operation, and readiness of facility test support systems including cryogenic, fluid, structural, and high-pressure liquid/gas systems required to conduct propulsion test activities at NASA's Stennis Space Center.

title: Electrical Engineer, AST, Electrical Experimental Equipment (Direct Hire)
description: You will serve as an AST Electrical Engineer in the Test Operations Branch of the Engineering and Test Directorate at NASA's Stennis Space Center. You will support the planning, design, and development of electrical equipment and systems used to operate and control specialized research and development facilities, test equipment, and process systems.

title: Program Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: Serves as a Program Specialist in the Engineering and Test Directorate at NASA's Stennis Space Center, supporting propulsion test operations by ensuring the operational readiness, reliability, and alignment of technical systems with test objectives, requirements, and facility capabilities.

title: Supervisory Program Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: Serves as a Supervisory Program Specialist responsible for leading and directing electrical and mechanical technicians in support of propulsion test activities at NASA's Stennis Space Center. Plans and prioritizes work assignments, allocates tasks based on project requirements, evaluates technician performance, and identifies training and development needs to ensure employees maintain technical proficiency and support safe, effective test operations.

title: Program Manager (Direct Hire)
description: Serves as a Project Manager within the Engineering and Test Directorate, providing project direction, establishing project management standards, and leading multidisciplinary teams in the planning, execution, and financial oversight of propulsion test projects and other mission-enabling initiatives across the directorate.

title: Lead Engineering Technician (Direct Hire)
description: As an Engineering technician in the Engineering and Test Directorate, you will be an integral member of the team responsible for performing propulsion test activities at Stennis Space Center. Your skills will be highly utilized to fabricate, install, activate and operate technical systems necessary for the successful achievement of the goals of the Center, NASA and our commercial partners.

title: Engineering Technician (Direct Hire)
description: As an Engineering technician in the Engineering and Test Directorate, you will be an integral member of the team responsible for performing propulsion test activities at Stennis Space Center. Your skills will be highly utilized to fabricate, install, activate and operate technical systems necessary for the successful achievement of the goals of the Center, NASA and our commercial partners.

title: Aerospace Engineer, AST, Aerospace Flight Systems (Direct Hire)
organization: George C. Marshall Space Flight Center
description: Serves as the Avionics Manager responsible for the design, development, integration, and operation of flight avionics systems in support of the Space Launch System (SLS) Program. Responsibilities encompass the full system lifecycle, including design and operational requirements definition, hardware and software development, testing, verification, safety, integration, certification, and operations in support of the Space Launch System (SLS) Program.

title: Registration Program Specialist
description: This position is located in the DEA, New England Field Division. This positions primary purpose is to ensure compliance and registration for all entities authorized to prescribe, manufacture, and/or distribute controlled substances. The incumbents will identify and resolve discrepancies and approve or deny registration applications against established DEA licensing criteria.

title: Supervisory Security Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: The Office of Protective Services, Personnel Security Division is seeking a dynamic individual to lead a team responsible for making adjudicative decisions for the Agency. You will develop and lead a group of Personnel Security Specialists and be responsible for ensuring a wide range of personnel security matters including the support and implementation of all Trusted Workforce 2.0 requirements, and ensuring security and adjudicative issues are resolved timely, thoroughly and accurately.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description: This position is located in the Division of Consumer Response and Operations (DCRO) in the Bureau of Consumer Protection (BCP) at the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) in Washington, D.C. The Bureau of Consumer Protection investigates and litigates civil actions to enforce federal consumer protection laws. For more information visit https://www.ftc.gov/about-ftc.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description: This position is located in the Division of Consumer Response and Operations (DCRO) in the Bureau of Consumer Protection (BCP) at the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) in Washington, D.C. The Bureau of Consumer Protection investigates and litigates civil actions to enforce federal consumer protection laws. For more information visit https://www.ftc.gov/about-ftc.

description: This position is located in the Division of Consumer Response and Operations (DCRO) in the Bureau of Consumer Protection (BCP) at the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) in Washington, D.C. description: This position is located in the Division of Consumer Response and Operations (DCRO) in the Bureau of Consumer Protection (BCP) at the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) in Washington, D.C. title: Supervisory Communications Specialist
description: This position is part of the Office of Science - Headquarters, Department of Energy. As a Supervisory Communications Specialist, you will be responsible for providing high-visibility products for public, industry, and industry stakeholders. Create innovative and visual messages to clearly communicate the Office of Science's mission and priorities. .

title: Director, Office of Procurement
organization: National Aeronautics and Space Administration - Agency Wide
description: The Office of Procurement is in search of two ambitious, highly motivated, and experienced leaders to serve as Director of Procurement at Marshall Space Flight Center (MSFC) and Kennedy Space Center (KSC). The incumbent will lead all procurement activities, including planning, budgeting, and execution. This announcement will be used to fill two vacancies - one at Marshall Space Flight Center and one at the Kennedy Space Center.

title: Supervisory Information Technology Specialist (Policy and Planning)
description: The Federal Law Enforcement Training Centers (FLETC) is looking for a talented Chief Information Officer/Assistant Director at FLETC's headquarters location in Glynco, Georgia. This position provides component-level strategic leadership for the direction, planning, management, and coordination of the component's information technology (IT), IT systems/program management, cybersecurity, information and technology accessibility, information resource management, and IT acquisition management.

title: IT Specialist (INFOSEC/ENTARCH)
description: This position is located at Department of Housing and Urban Development Office of Inspector General, Deputy Inspector General. As an IT Specialist (INFOSEC/ENTARCH), you will provide security, network, computing, capacity, policy development, desktop services, and mobile device technology to meet the requirements of around 500 users in about 40 offices across the country.

title: Supervisory Information Technology Program Manager
organization: Office of the Assistant Secretary for Administration and Management
description: The Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) serves as the IT hub of the U.S. Department of Labor. We develop, maintain and protect IT solutions and data across our 27 agencies to enable mission outcomes through technology and service. OCIO continually enhances federal IT and digital capability with a focus on cybersecurity and customer experience to serve America's wage earners, job seekers and retirees.

title: Space Scientist, AST, Planetary Studies (Direct Hire)
description: As the NASA Antarctic Meteorite Curator within the Astromaterials Acquisition and Curation Office at the NASA Johnson Space Center (JSC), you will lead the documentation, allocation, and security of the portion of the US Antarctic Meteorite Collection housed at NASA JSC. You will perform curation activities in support of the US Antarctic Meteorite Collection and other existing and emerging collections as required.

title: Director, Center for Infrastructure Systems and Technology
organization: Office of the Secretary of Transportation
description: This position leads and oversees the Center for Infrastructure Systems and Technology within the Department of Transportation's Office of the Assistant Secretary for Research and Technology (OST-R), John A. Volpe National Transportation Center (Volpe), located in Cambridge, MA. The position is comprised of seven technical divisions with a combined federal staffing of more than 103 employees, a portfolio of 166 projects, and annual obligations of over $81million, on average.

title: SUPERVISORY INTELLIGENCE SPECIALIST
description: You will serve as a GG-0132-14 Supervisory Intelligence Specialist as the Branch Head within the Geospatial Intelligence Science and Analysis Division (GSD) and Subject Matter Expert (SME) on Full Spectrum Geospatial Intelligence (GEOINT), where you will plan, direct, and supervise GEOINT activities for the Marine Corps Intelligence Activity, HQ US MARINE CORPS in Quantico, VA.

title: Telecommunications Specialist
description: Organizational Location: This position is with the Department of Homeland Security, within U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Office of Information and Technology, Field Support Directorate, Northwest Central Region, located in Grand Forks, ND. Additional selections may be made beyond the total number of vacancies specified using this vacancy announcement. Further selections may also be made for additional organizational divisions and/or units within the duty location(s) listed above.

title: Information Technology Specialist (Artificial Intelligence)
description: Organizational Location: This position is with the Department of Homeland Security, within U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Office of Information and Technology, Chief Technology Office, located in Ashburn, VA..

title: Data Scientist
description: This position is located within the Office of Research, Development, and Technology (HRT) at the Turner-Fairbank Highway Research Center (TFHRC). HRT leads a nationally coordinated highway research and technology program to enable improved safety, durability, resilience & operational performance of the Nation's highway system; champions the advancement of highway innovation in support of strategic goals & objectives; & provides expert technical assistance on matters of national importance.

title: Program Analyst (Informatics)
description: The incumbent serves as a Program Analyst (Health Informatics) located at VA Puget Sound Health Care System within the Office of Health Informatics. The primary purpose of the position is to improve the quality, efficiency, and effectiveness of health care by integrating clinical and information processes necessary to achieve successful adoption and application of new technologies throughout the VHA.

title: IT Specialist (Artificial Intelligence) DIRECT HIRE
description: The U.S. International Trade Commission is an independent Federal agency that provides the President and Congress with high-quality analysis and technical support on international trade, tariff and competitiveness issues; investigates and makes determinations in proceedings involving imports claimed to injure a domestic industry or violate U.S. intellectual property rights; and maintains the U.S. Harmonized Tariff Schedule.

title: Emergency Management Specialist
description: This position is part of the Office of Management, Department of Energy. As an Emergency Management Specialist, you will independently plan and manage the program, which includes developing and implementing Occupant Emergency Plans, leading training and exercises, and coordinating response activities. You will serve as the Continuity of Operation (COOP)/Continuity of Government (COG) for the Office of Management at the Department of Energy Headquarters.

title: Security Specialist (Direct Hire)
description: This position is located in the Office of Protective services, Langley Research Center Hampton Virginia. As an Information and Industrial Security Specialist, you will plan, analyze, develop, and implement Center security policies, standards and procedures and represent Langley Research Center to boards, committees and working groups. You will be required to serve as an Armed Federal Agent and complete the Federal Arrest Authority Academy.

title: Sr. Geotechnical Engineer
description: This position is located in Division of Engineering. The supervisor is Raj Iyengar. This position IS subject to Confidential Financial Disclosure reporting requirements. This position IS subject to security ownership restriction reporting requirements.

title: Management and Program Analyst
description: This position is part of the Office of the Chief Human Capital Officer, Department of Energy. As a Management and Program Analyst, you will manage the internal business operations for the Office of Workforce Administration and Management. The role involves planning and implementing initiatives, overseeing correspondence, including, coordinating responses to inquiries and data calls from various government agencies, and managing administrative and operational needs.

title: Student Trainee (Operations Research)
organization: US Army Civilian Human Resources Agency
description: About the Position: The Student Intern Program provides selectees an exciting opportunity to embark on a training and development path for a successful career as an Army Civilian employee. Students may also receive payment for tuition/training, which is subject to availability, and potential conversion into full-time positions upon graduation. Don't wait! Apply now to start your Army Civilian internship today! 1st Year Salary $36,464- $44,780 2nd Year Salary $37,432-$48,665

title: Supervisory IT Specialist
description: This position is part of the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation (DFC),Office of Information Technology (OIT). As a Supervisory IT Specialist you will be responsible for all aspects of the development, modernization, and enhancement of systems and software that directly support the DFC mission.

title: Attorney-Adviser
description: You will serve as an Attorney-Adviser (Employment and General Law) within the NASA Office of the General Counsel (OGC) at Lyndon B. Johnson Space Center (JSC). You will focus on federal employment law, including providing counseling and advice to senior managers and defending judicial and administrative complaints of employment discrimination, with federal sector litigation in multiple venues. You will also perform other general law duties.

description: This position is located in the Office of Nuclear Reactor Regulation, Division of Engineering, Reactor Engineering Branch. The supervisor is Raj Iyengar. organization: Office of the Director of National Intelligence

description: The Policy & Capabilities (PC) Directorate ensures the IC is best postured for the threats and challenges of an uncertain future, through strategy, policy, and capabilities development. title: Mission and Operation Support Specialist
description: This position is located in the DEA, Gulfport, MS District Office This position(s) primary purpose is to support the Outlying Office (OO) related to GSU and FPU level BOS activities involving: administrative, human resource liaison, fleet management, personal property (accountable and non-accountable) management, security (personnel, physical, and information/communication), inspections/internal control, logistics, TFO management, Imprest, and records management.

title: Mission Support Specialist
description: This position is located in the DEA, Birmingham District Office This position's primary purpose is to exercise, independent judgment and discretion in assisting in the preparation and processing of confidential employee matters. The incumbent will also maintain and coordinate the administrative support program in the Business Operations Section by initiating and ensuring effective communication, coordination, and follow-up action with other Divisions and Headquarters Program Offices within DEA.

title: Education Policy Analyst, AD-1701-00, GS-15 Equivalent (Excepted Service) (Term NTE 3 YRS) (Assistant Director, NAEP Policy)
description: This position is located in the National Assessment Governing Board (NAGB), in Washington, DC. The National Assessment Governing Board (NAGB) sets policy for the National Assessment of Education Progress (NAEP).

title: Attorney Advisor (General)
description: This position is located in the Drug Enforcement Administrator (DEA), Office of Chief Counsel. This position primary purpose is to provide legal advice, review disciplinary proposals, defend DEA in employment-related administrative litigation, and support the defense of DEA and its employees in federal lawsuits. Department of Justice (DOJ) agencies post experienced attorney job announcements on the DOJ Careers page.

title: Supervisory, AST Flight Systems Operations
description: As a branch chief in the JSC/Flight Operations Directorate (FOD) Extravehicular Activities (EVA), Robotics, and Crew Operations Division (CX) you will lead, supervise, and develop a team creating operational concepts, plans, procedures, and executing real time support for human spaceflight missions, including training of flight controllers and astronauts.

title: Community Planner
description: As a Community Planner within the Facilities and Real Estate Division, Office of Strategic Infrastructure (OSI) you will provide support to the agency's facilities and real property management function including facilities planning, design, construction, maintenance, operation, resource and budget, and real estate. You will lead the development of the Agency Master Plan, leading the Master Planning Community of Practice and assisting the Centers with their Master Plans.

title: Management and Program Analyst (AI Program Manager)
description: This position is with the U. S. Department of Labor, Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), within the Directorate of Administrative Programs (DAP), in the Office of Technology Assistance (OTA), located in Arlington Heights, IL, or Washington, D.C. This position is at the full performance level. This position is inside the bargaining unit. This position is not remote. Selectee will be expected to report to one of the offices above.

title: Electronics Engineer, AST, Data Hardware Systems
description: Serves as a Lead Communications Engineer within Grounds Systems Integration Branch of the Launch Services Program (LSP) Office, located at Kennedy Space Center (KSC).

title: Information Systems Security Engineer - Mid to Expert Level (Maryland)
description: Are you a cyber professional with the drive and expertise to be on the forefront of the cyber fight; tackling NSA's complex mission to defend against cyber threats of today and tomorrow? NSA, the nation's leading cyber agency, has exciting and challenging positions in Cyber Security Engineering and Cyber and TEMPEST vulnerability analysis/mitigation. Are you ready to help secure our Nation's critical Infrastructure? If so, NSA is the place for you!

title: Management Analyst
description: This position is located in the DEA, Operations Division, Office of Training (OT), Academy Operations Section (OTA), General Support Unit (OTAG). This position(s) primary purpose is to serve as a Management Analyst and primary Human Resources Liaison to the Office of Training staff, providing guidance and support on a wide range of HR functions. Advises managers and staff on personnel actions, benefits, performance management, staffing and employee programs.

title: Program Analyst
description: This position is located in the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA), Bogota, Colombia Country Office. This position primary purpose is to enforce the controlled substances laws and regulations of the United States and bring to the criminal and civil justice system of the United States, or any other competent jurisdiction.

locations: Port of Spain, Trinidad and Tobago

title: Research Highway Engineer - DIRECT HIRE
description: This position is in the Office of Research, Development, and Technology at the Turner-Fairbank Highway Research Center in McLean, Virginia. This position serves as a Communications Engineer on the Safety Design and Operations Team specializing in highway engineering research related to wireless technologies for Connected Vehicles and the application of communications to support the safe operation of emerging technologies such as automated vehicles, digital infrastructure, and AI.

title: Supervisory Aerospace Engineer, AST, Aerospace Flight Systems
description: You will serve as the Life Support Office Manager with the ISS Vehicle Office at Johnson Space Center. You will manage a highly complex and critical scope of technical work as well as supervision of employees. The position manages sustaining of ISS vehicle system hardware, developing future life support technologies, and supporting ISS logistics, sparing, and parts management. The hardware development skill set required for this position is a core, critical Agency competency.

title: Program Manager
organization: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
description: Lead proactive counterintelligence and security activities across the United States government, integrating the instruments of national power to detect, report, deter, disrupt, investigate, exploit, and defeat foreign intelligence threats.

organization: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
description: Lead proactive counterintelligence and security activities across the United States government, integrating the instruments of national power to detect, report, deter, disrupt, investigate, exploit, and defeat foreign intelligence threats.

description: This position is located in Division of Engineering. The supervisor is Raj Iyengar. This position IS subject to Confidential Financial Disclosure reporting requirements. This position IS subject to security ownership restriction reporting requirements.

title: INTERDISCIPLINARY General Engineer/Physical Scientist (Recent Graduate)
description: The Recent Graduates Program is a dynamic, one-year developmental program that promotes careers in the Federal Government to recent graduates. Participants in the Program receive training and professional development, complete an Individual Development Plan (IDP), and are assigned a mentor. Recent Graduates who successfully complete Program requirements may be eligible for non-competitive conversion to a term or permanent position in the civil service.

title: Accountant (Recent Graduate)
description: The Recent Graduates Program is a dynamic, one-year developmental program that promotes careers in the Federal Government to recent graduates. Participants in the Program receive training and professional development, complete an Individual Development Plan (IDP), and are assigned a mentor. Recent Graduates who successfully complete Program requirements may be eligible for non-competitive conversion to a term or permanent position in the civil service.

title: Director of Systems Engineering and Integration
description: Serves as the Director of the Systems Engineering and Integration (SE&I) Office for the Moon to Mars (M2M) Program within the Exploration Space Development Mission Directorate. The SE&I Director assists the Deputy Associate Administrator, M2M Program in the leadership and integration across the Moon to Mars Program.

title: IT Specialist
description: As an Information Technology (IT) Specialist in the Federal government, you will perform duties related to the installation and configuration of computer hardware, software, operating systems, networks, applications, and printers and scanners; providing technical support and troubleshooting issues; assisting with the maintenance of computer systems and networks; and ensuring the application of appropriate IT security measures.

title: Senior Scientist for Lunar Exploration
description: The Senior Scientist for Lunar Exploration within the Science Mission Directorate serves as the principal advisor to the Deputy Associate Administrator for Exploration and Lunar Discovery and Exploration Program, on all science-related aspects of NASA's lunar science and exploration initiatives; advises on the scientific strategy for the exploration of Earth's Moon; and works with programmatic management to ensure the implementation of the science content is optimized.

title: Senior Scientist for Lunar Exploration
description: The Senior Scientist for Lunar Exploration within the Science Mission Directorate serves as the principal advisor to the Deputy Associate Administrator for Exploration and Lunar Discovery and Exploration Program, on all science-related aspects of NASA's lunar science and exploration initiatives; advises on the scientific strategy for the exploration of Earth's Moon; and works with programmatic management to ensure the implementation of the science content is optimized.

title: Deputy Chief Human Capital Officer for Policy and Programs
organization: Office of the Secretary of the Interior
description: The Office of Human Capital (OHC) drives the Department of the Interior's workforce strategy, ensuring the right talent supports mission success. The position reports to the Deputy Assistant Secretary for Human Capital, Learning and Safety and Chief Human Capital Officer within the Office of Policy, Management, and Budget. This is a Category 3 SES position; minimum pay is $197,200, with final salary based on current salary and other factors. Veterans' preference does not apply to the SES.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
organization: Department of the Air Force - Agency Wide
description: This is a Non-Tenure Track position. Serves as Postdoctoral Researcher to substantially increase faculty productivity and improve AFIT educational and research products. Postdoctoral researchers provide a unique opportunity to educate civilian human capital for the Air Force workforce thereby meeting Air Force STEM human capital needs.

title: Chief Technology Officer
description: This Senior Level (SL) position is located in the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), Office of Inspector General (OIG), Office of Management and Policy (OMP), Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO). This position will be located in an HHS OIG office in the United States, to be determined upon selection. Learn more about this agency

title: General Engineer/Physical Scientist
description: This position is part of the Office of Electricity, Department of Energy. As a General Engineer/Physical Scientist, you will manage the North American Energy Resilience Model (NAERM), providing thought leadership in the area of grid modeling, assist in constructing new models and tools for addressing grid related questions, maintaining internal model and data operations, and develop and implement new analysis approaches and methods with limited oversight from the Director of Grid Modeling.

title: Executive Officer - Secure Enclave IC Support Element
organization: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
description: The Policy & Capabilities (PC) Directorate ensures the IC is best postured for the threats and challenges of an uncertain future, through strategy, policy, and capabilities development. PC is comprised of the Strategic Capabilities Office (SCO) and Policy Strategy and Accountability (PSA) Office.

title: Executive Officer - Secure Enclave IC Support Element
organization: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
description: The Policy & Capabilities (PC) Directorate ensures the IC is best postured for the threats and challenges of an uncertain future, through strategy, policy, and capabilities development. PC is comprised of the Strategic Capabilities Office (SCO) and Policy Strategy and Accountability (PSA) Office.

title: Assistant Inspector General for Technology Solutions Division
description: The U.S. Small Business Administration, Office of Inspector General (OIG) is seeking an exceptional leader who will direct the Technology Solutions Division of the OIG. We promote open and honest communication to create a positive work environment.

title: Contracting Officer's Representative (Procurement Technical Analyst)
description: This position is located in the DEA, Diversion Control Division, Procurement and Contracting Unit (DPAC). This position(s) primary purpose is ensuring that the Diversion Control Program (DCP) goals are supported through efficient, compliant, and effective contracting processes. The Contracting Officer's Representative ensures that contractor deliverables are met on time, that the terms of contracts are adhered to, and that any issues arising during contract execution are addressed promptly.

title: Attorney Advisor
organization: Administrative Office of the U.S. Courts
description: The Attorney Advisor position is in the Department of Program Services, Judicial Services Office (JSO), Judicial Programs Division. JSO provides support, services, and programs addressing the administrative, governance, and policy needs of federal judges, Article III courts, territorial district courts, Court of Federal Claims, bankruptcy courts, and magistrate judges and their staff.

title: PEGA Developer- DHA
description: IT Specialist (APPSW), PEGA Developer position(s) are being filled through the Office of Personnel Management's delegated Direct Hire Authority, open to all U.S. citizens. Selections made under this bulletin will be processed as new appointments to the civil service. Current civil service employees would, therefore, be given new appointments to the civil service. Under the provisions of the Direct Hire Authority, Veterans Preference and the "Rule of Many" do not apply.

title: IT Specialist (AI Architect)
description: The Division of Administration (DA or Division) directs the innovative internal management of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's (CFTC or Commission) business, human resources, financial, technological, security, and strategic operational resources. The Division's role is to effectively and efficiently ensure the fulfillment of the Commission's mission through continued success in continuity of operations.

Selections made under this bulletin will be processed as new appointments to the civil service. Current civil service employees would, therefore, be given new appointments to the civil service. Under the provisions of the Direct Hire Authority, Veterans Preference and the "Rule of Many" do not apply.

title: Diversion Investigator
description: TOUGH WORK. VITAL MISSION. Challenge yourself! You have the power to help combat drug trafficking. Be a part of DEA! It's tough work, but a vital mission. Whatever your background or expertise, your work at DEA will be tremendously rewarding because it will have a daily impact on national security and the quality of life of all Americans. The salary listed is based on the 2026 Base Salary table and will be augmented by locality pay as authorized for the duty location of the position.

title: Trial Attorney
description: This is a public notice flyer. Applications will not be accepted through USA Jobs. If you are interested in applying, please see the official announcement on the DOJs Legal Careers page by following the links below: Legal Careers | Trial Attorney (Chapter 11) | United States Department of Justice https://www.justice.gov/legal-careers/job/trial-attorney-chapter-11
